title: Port existing code to a WASM target
description: Identify host dependencies that do not exist in WASM and replace them with imports or runtime-appropriate shims.
body: Steps: 1. Inventory the code's host dependencies: filesystem, threads, sockets, time, randomness, environment. 2. Determine which the target runtime provides (WASI offers a capability-scoped subset; the browser provides none of these directly). 3. Replace unavailable calls with imported JS functions (browser) or WASI APIs, or feature-gate them out. 4. Handle threading explicitly (WASM threads need shared memory and cross-origin isolation; many builds are single-threaded). 5. Build, instantiate, and exercise the ported paths. Rollback point: the port is on a branch. Validation: the module instantiates with all imports satisfied and the ported paths behave correctly in the target runtime.
